opinion.h: NELSON ET AL. v. TENNESSEE. CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF TENNESSEE, EASTERN DISTRICT. No. 56. Argued December 12, 1957. Decided December 16, 1957.
opinion.p: Certiorari dismissed for want of properly presented federal question.
Hobart F. Atkins argued the cause and filed a brief for petitioners.
James M. Glasgow, Assistant Attorney General of Tennessee, argued the cause for respondent. With him on the brief was George F. McCanless, Attorney General.
The writ of certiorari is dismissed for want of a properly presented federal question.
opinion.h: MACDONALD v. LA SALLE NATIONAL BANK, CONSERVATOR, 355 U.S. 271 (1957)
opinion.p: 355 U.S. 271 MACDONALD v. LA SALLE NATIONAL BANK, CONSERVATOR. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 504. Decided December 16, 1957.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
John R. Nicholson and Charles M. Nisen for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
